seed_problem: Partition a weighted graph into two balanced halves maximizing cut weight; checker validates balance and scores cut/baseline.
mutation: composite: keep the balanced max-cut goal but tighten the output to an EXACT balanced bisection (n/2 per side, not just approximately balanced) and generalize the input to a planted-bipartition-plus-frustration weighted (multi)graph, so recovering the optimal balanced bisection is NP-hard and greedy vs. swap-local-search vs. multi-restart strategies diverge.
title: Twin-Refinery Belt Split: Balanced Ore-Conflict Separation
strategies: alternating-dock baseline: assign asteroid i to refinery (i mod 2); always balanced, cuts about half of the heavy cross weight
weight-greedy construction: place asteroids in order of decreasing incident weight, each to the refinery that maximizes marginal separation bonus while respecting the n/2 capacity
pairwise-swap local search: from a greedy start, repeatedly swap one asteroid from each refinery whenever it raises the total bonus, until no improving swap remains
seeded multi-restart search: run swap local search from many random balanced starts and keep the best balanced bisection found
